text:
Market today: Mixed and nervous. The S&P is at a technical crossroads per today's news, Fed officials are split on inflation (Goolsbee hawkish, Williams dovish), and Microsoft is in a historic June slide on spending concerns - that's a drag on the whole tech complex including your chip and software names.

Your holdings: PLTR is down -17.56% and flagged SELL - on margin that hole is getting expensive, no fundamental catalyst here to bail you out. AVGO is your largest position at -13.18% and flagged TRIM - that's a big dollar drawdown amplified by leverage, needs sizing attention now. SPAL is -16.49% on a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily - tactical instruments bleed fast when they go wrong, treat this as urgent. SPCX is a brand-new IPO with almost no price history; margin plus illiquidity here is a real risk.

Watch: Whether SPY breaks its critical support level (your stop is 705) - a confirmed break likely drags everything you own lower hard. Also watch AVGO for any further deterioration given its size in your book.

Bottom line: You have three positions actively bleeding on margin right now - PLTR, AVGO, and SPAL all need a decision today, not tomorrow.
